pollination.note: Classic obligate-mutualism — moth pollinates only yucca, larva eats only yucca seeds.
Each Ficus species coevolves with one wasp species; arity grows to 2 wasps over the fig life cycle.
Monarch larvae also EAT milkweed; the adult re-pollinates as nectar feeder.
Year-round in tropics; bill shape matches the flower curve.
California almond bloom hosts the largest managed-pollinator migration on earth.
Bumblebees sonicate the anther — honeybees can't buzz-pollinate tomatoes.
Lesser long-nosed bats feed on saguaro nectar by night, doves take over by day.
Hawkmoth pollination — nocturnal, white flowers, strong scent.
Beetles predate bees: magnolia retains the ancient cantharophily syndrome.
Birds, not bees: African aloes coevolved with long-billed sunbirds.
Primate pollination — lemurs push apart the bract to drink nectar.
Honeyeaters are the dominant pollinator of the Banksia genus.
Sexual deception — flower mimics female bee; male bee pseudo-copulates and disperses pollen.
Floating water lilies trap beetles overnight, release with pollen at dawn.
Whistling thorn acacia: bees pollinate while the resident ants defend the tree (see mutualism).

mutualism.note: Clownfish lives in the anemone's stinging tentacles; both fully dependent.
Cleaner wrasse picks parasites from grouper mouth — predator–prey roles invert.
Oxpecker eats ticks off the rhino; some studies argue parasitism too (wound-feeding).
Fungus extends the root system 100×; pine gives sugars — both die alone.
Lichen-style; alga photosynthesises, fungus protects + provides minerals.
Bleaching = breakdown of this single mutualism — the global crisis driver.
Acacia grows hollow swollen thorns; ants defend the tree against giraffes.
Egret rides cattle; cattle stir up insects the egret eats.
Honeyguide leads honey badger to hives; both feast on what the badger breaks open.
Ants 'farm' aphids: protection in exchange for honeydew.
Termite cannot digest cellulose alone; flagellate cannot survive outside the termite.
Rhizobia convert atmospheric N₂ into ammonia inside soybean root nodules.
Sloth fur grows algae and fungi — supplemental food and camouflage.
Pistol shrimp digs the burrow; goby stands lookout with better vision.
Lichens and mycorrhizal fungi cooperate in soil networks — community-scale mutualism.

parasitism.note: Cuckoo lays a single egg in warbler's nest; chick ejects host eggs.
Zombie-ant fungus alters ant behaviour to climb and die in a fungal-friendly spot.
Mistletoe punctures bark, taps host xylem for water + minerals.
Strangler fig germinates in canopy, sends roots down, eventually kills host tree.
Sea lamprey attaches with rasping disc; one of the worst invasive in the Great Lakes.
Beef tapeworm: cysts in muscle, adult in human gut — humans complete the cycle.
Malaria parasite uses mosquito as vector; humans are the other host (we don't include human entity).
Cat is the definitive host; rodent intermediate is behaviour-manipulated to seek cats.
Botfly lays eggs on a vector insect; larva burrows under skin.
Hammerhead flatworm preys on earthworms (technically predator, behaves like parasite).
Black-legged tick vectors Lyme disease — three-host life cycle.
Hairworm grows inside cricket, then makes it leap into water so the worm can emerge.

predation.note: Lions hunt cooperatively; success rate higher than any other big cat.
Famous breaching predation at Seal Island, False Bay — peaks in winter.
Salmon spawning run concentrates predation; eagle is opportunist.
1995 wolf reintroduction reshaped elk behaviour AND willow growth — trophic cascade.
Polar bear ambushes ringed seal at breathing holes — sea ice is the platform.
Punta Norte intentional stranding — orcas hunt sea lions on the beach itself.
Cheetah is the fastest sprinter; daytime hunter to avoid larger nocturnal cats.
Snow leopard preys on bharal at 3,000–5,000 m elevation.
Sambar is the primary prey of Bengal tigers; both are forest-edge specialists.
Anaconda ambushes capybara at water's edge; constricts and swallows whole.
Komodo dragon uses venomous bite + endurance — buffalo dies of shock days later.
Fastest animal — diving stoop reaches 320 km/h, kills with talons mid-flight.
Mantis shrimp's strike accelerates faster than a 22-caliber bullet.
Keystone species: otter predation on urchin keeps kelp forest from becoming urchin barren.
Apex of apex: a single orca pair drove great whites out of False Bay 2017.

food_webs.name: Yellowstone food web
food_webs.note: 1995 wolf reintroduction is the canonical trophic-cascade story: wolves cut elk browsing, willows recovered, beavers returned.

food_webs.name: California kelp forest food web
food_webs.note: Keystone trio: kelp (producer), urchin (herbivore), otter (predator). Remove the otter, urchins explode, kelp vanishes.

food_webs.name: Serengeti food web
food_webs.note: Great Migration: ~1.5M wildebeest move clockwise around the ecosystem each year, predator densities track them.

food_webs.name: Indo-Pacific coral reef food web
food_webs.note: Zooxanthellae make this whole ecosystem possible: 90% of the coral's energy is photosynthate from the algal symbiont.

food_webs.name: Arctic food web
food_webs.note: Sea ice is the keystone HABITAT — every node here is being squeezed by its retreat.

food_webs.name: Amazon rainforest food web
food_webs.note: Brazil nut tree depends on agouti (only mammal that can crack its pod) AND on a specific orchid bee for pollination — three-way mutualism.
